title: caltha leptosepala subsp. biflora
intro: Caltha leptosepala subsp. biflora, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Caltha leptosepala subsp. biflora include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and soothe skin irritations. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity.
